title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
The Indian government offers different types of investment schemes. Some schemes offer market-linked returns while others offer fixed interest income. The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) is a government-sponsored savings scheme designed for senior citizens, specifically individuals aged 60 years and above. Let’s understand the scheme in detail.
What is the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme?
The Senior Citizens' Savings Scheme (SCSS), backed by the government, is a fixed-income scheme designed for individuals aged 60 years or older. The scheme aims to provide financial security to retired individuals by paying a steady income. You can opt for the SCSS scheme individually or jointly with another individual and create a source of fixed income.
How Does the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Work?
Here’s how the saving scheme for senior citizens works –
Eligible individuals can open an SCSS account by making one deposit of between ₹1,000 and ₹30 lakhs.
For individuals depositing their retirement benefits into the scheme, the benefit must be invested within one month of receipt.
If the deposits exceed the maximum ceiling, the money is refunded instantly to the account holder.
The interest is calculated for the period that starts from the deposit date and ends on the 31st of March, 30th June, 30th September, or 31st December, whichever is the earliest.
The interest is deposited in the SCSS account at the starting date of April, July, October, and January.
The facility of premature closure is allowed through an application made in Form 2. However, a penalty is levied on such closure.
The SCSS account has a tenure of 5 years, which can be extended for another 3 years.
What is the SCSS Calculator?
The SCSS calculator is an online tool that enables investors to estimate the returns on their investments. All you have to do is enter the deposit details in the SCSS calculator online, and you will get the maturity amount and the interest earned
SCSS Return Calculator Formula
The formula to calculate the returns from the SCSS account is as follows -
Maturity amount: A = P x (1 + r/n) ^ (n x t)
Interest amount: CI = P x (1 + r/n) ^ (n x t) – P
Were,
P = principal,
r = interest rate,
n = compounding frequency (4 quarters),
and t = tenure in years.
Features of Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
The salient features of the Sr. Citizen Saving Scheme are as follows –
Interest rate changes on a quarterly basis
SCSS interest rates are reviewed quarterly by the government to ensure they align with current market conditions. The interest income is fixed, guaranteeing the returns that you get from the scheme. Additionally, the interest is compounded and paid quarterly, providing investors with a steady source of income.
Deposit Range: Minimum & Maximum
The minimum deposit is ₹1,000, which can be invested in multiples of this amount, and the maximum deposit ceiling is ₹ 30 lakhs. The limit for cash deposits is ₹1 lakh; any amount exceeding this limit must be paid using the bank's payment methods. The deposits can be made from the amount received from the retirement benefits. The sum should be deposited within one month of receiving retirement benefits. All deposits over the ceiling limit are refunded instantly to the account holders.
Investment duration
The scheme has a five-year maturity. Investors have the option of a three-year extension, which gives them flexibility to save further. The extension applications should be done within a year of the maturity date. The transfer of accounts between post offices and banks is also permitted, making it convenient for account holders who may need to relocate during the investment period.
Early withdrawal and account closure terms
Early closing can be done at any time by submitting an application in Form 2; however, penalties are imposed depending on the time of closing. When it is closed within one year, all the interest paid is deducted from the principal amount. A penalty of 1.5% of the principal amount is imposed in case of closure between one and two years, and a 1% penalty applies if you close the account after two years.
Pre-mature withdrawal can only be done once, as long as the scheme is active. It is worth noting that, since August 29, 2024, withdrawals made from SCSS accounts are exempt from tax.
Facility to add a nominee
Account holders are allowed to nominate individuals at the time of account opening or at any time thereafter. This allows easier transfer of proceeds if the depositor passes away. Investors can add one or more nominees without restrictions.
Benefits of the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
A senior citizen savings scheme is a good retirement investment option because it offers multiple benefits to elderly citizens. Some of these benefits are as follows:
Tax-saving benefits
The investments in SCSS are allowed as tax deductions under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act if you choose the old regime. The maximum deduction is limited to ₹1.5 lakhs in a financial year. The interest received is subject to tax as per the taxpayer's income tax bracket. The proceeds received on premature withdrawal are tax-free. Tax deducted at source (TDS) will apply to interest income if it exceeds ₹50,000 in a financial year for individuals under 60 and ₹1 lakh for senior citizens.
Attractive return rate
The interest rate on the SCSS scheme, effective until 31st March 2026, stands at 8.20% p.a. This competitive rate offers senior citizens attractive returns on their investments. The interest is also reviewed quarterly to factor in market dynamics.
Government-secured savings scheme
As a government-backed investment plan, SCSS offers capital protection and guaranteed interest at the time of maturity. Since the scheme is backed by the government, there is no scope for default risk, making it one of the most secure and trustworthy investments among elderly citizens.
Low-risk investment option
Compared to the market-linked instruments, which are volatile, SCSS offers predictable and fixed returns irrespective of market fluctuations. The guaranteed interest rates and capital security make SCSS suitable for elderly investors who may be risk-averse and seek a secure and steady income.
Eligibility Criteria of the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
The eligibility criteria of the Senior Citizen Saving Scheme are as follows -
Accounts can be opened by Indian citizens who are aged 60 years and older.
Those aged between 55 and 60 years who are retired under the Superannuation, Special Voluntary Retirement Scheme (VRS), or VRS are also eligible for the scheme.
In the case of retired defence personnel, except civilian employees in the defence sector, the scheme is available after the completion of 50 years of age if they meet other specified criteria.
Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs) and Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) are not eligible for the scheme.
The deposit amount ranges from ₹ 1,000 to ₹30 lakhs in multiples of ₹ 1,000.
The eligible retirement benefits that can be deposited include the following –
Gratuity
Provident Fund
Commuted pension
The saving component of the Group Savings Linked Insurance Scheme
Leave encashment
The benefit of the Employees’ Family Pension Scheme
Ex-gratia payment received from VRS or special VRS
Documents Required to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account
The applicants will be required to submit the following documents:
Know your customer (KYC) documents, such as an Aadhaar card, PAN card, voter’s ID card, or passport.
Utility bills, such as electricity or telephone bills, are often used as proof of address.
A birth certificate or senior citizen card may be needed in certain situations to verify the depositor’s age.
The application is to be accompanied by two recent passport-size photographs to finalise the account opening process.
How to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Account
You can open the SCSS account both offline and online. The offline and online processes are explained below -
Offline process of opening a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account
Visit a bank branch or post office of your choice.
Get the official SCSS application form from the counter and fill it out, stating the correct personal and investment-related information.
Include self-attested copies of all the mandatory documents and two passport-size photographs.
Additionally, deposit the amount you wish to invest in the scheme. The bank or post office would verify your application and open the SCSS account.
Online process of opening a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account
Eligible investors can open an SCSS account online, provided their bank offers a net banking facility for account opening. To open the account -
Log in to your net banking portal, and under the 'Investment' or 'Savings' menu, select SCSS.
Fill out the online application form with the necessary information
Attach scanned copies of the necessary documents and deposit the investment amount online
After verification, the SCSS account would be opened.
Conclusion
The Senior Citizen Saving Scheme can help older individuals plan a comfortable life after retirement by creating a source of guaranteed and regular income. Investors can invest their retirement benefits in the scheme and earn attractive returns on a quarterly basis. The tax benefit under Section 80C on investments and the tax exemption on premature withdrawals provide added benefits.
So, if you are eligible, you can invest in the scheme to generate a fixed income that can help you manage your expenses post-retirement. Understand what the SCSS scheme is all about and invest in the savings plan if it aligns with your financial goals.
Key Takeaways
SCSS is a government-backed savings scheme for senior citizens that offers guaranteed and regular interest income.
The one-time deposit amount starts from ₹1000 and goes up to ₹30 lakhs.
The maturity period is 5 years and can be extended by up to three years.
Some of the benefits of the SCSS scheme include liquidity through premature withdrawal, guaranteed returns, and tax benefits under Section 80C, among others.
FAQs
What happens to the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme after the 5-year maturity period?
After the 5-year maturity period, the SCSS account matures, and you can withdraw your deposit. However, you would have the option to extend the scheme's tenure by another 3 years if you apply within one year of the maturity date.
Are there penalty charges for partial withdrawals under SCSS?
Yes, there are penalty charges that depend on when you make the withdrawal. The charges are as follows –
Withdrawal within the first year – Interest paid till withdrawal would be deducted from the principal amount
Withdrawal after a year but before 2 years - 1.5% of the deposited amount
Withdrawal after two years - 1% of the deposited amount
What is the current SCSS interest rate?
The current rate of SCSS interest is 8.2% per annum, which remains valid until 31st March 2026.
Can I invest ₹30 lakh in the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme?
Yes, you can invest ₹30 lakhs in SCSS. However, the deposit amount is limited to the retirement benefits received.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility

Senior Citizen Savings Scheme
The Indian government offers different types of investment schemes. Some schemes offer market- linked returns while others offer fixed interest income. The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) is a government-sponsored savings scheme designed for older individuals. Let’s understand the scheme in detail.
What is the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme?
The Senior Citizens' Savings Scheme (SCSS), backed by the government, is a fixed-income scheme designed for individuals aged 60 years or older. The scheme aims to provide financial security to retired individuals by paying a steady income. You can opt for the SCSS scheme individually or jointly with another individual and create a source of fixed income.
How Does the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Work? Here’s how the saving scheme for senior citizens works –
1. Eligible individuals can open an SCSS account by making one deposit of between ₹1,000 and
₹30 lakhs1. 2. For individuals depositing their retirement benefits into the scheme, the benefit must be
invested within one month of receipt1. 3. If the deposits exceed the maximum ceiling, the money is refunded instantly to the account
holder1. 4. The interest is calculated for the period that starts from the deposit date and ends on the
31st of March, 30th June, 30th September, or 31st December, whichever is the earliest2. 5. The interest is deposited in the SCSS account at the starting date of April, July, October, and
January2. 6. The facility of premature closure is allowed through an application made in Form 2.
However, a penalty is levied on such closure. 7. The SCSS account has a tenure of 5 years, which can be extended for another 3 years.
What is the SCSS Calculator? The SCSS calculator is an online tool that enables investors to estimate the returns on their investments. All you have to do is enter the deposit details in the SCSS calculator online, and you will get the maturity amount and the interest earned.2
SCSS Return Calculator Formula
The formula to calculate the returns from the SCSS account is as follows2 -
Maturity amount: A = P x (1 + r/n) ^ (n x t) Interest amount: CI = P x (1 + r/n) ^ (n x t) – P
Were, P = principal, r = interest rate, n = compounding frequency (4 quarters), and t = tenure in years. Features of Senior Citizen Savings Scheme The salient features of the Senior. Citizen Saving Scheme are as follows –
1. Interest rate changes on a quarterly basis
SCSS interest rates are reviewed quarterly by the government to ensure they align with current market conditions. The interest income is fixed, guaranteeing the returns that you get from the scheme4. Additionally, the interest is compounded and paid quarterly, providing investors with a steady source of income1. 2. Deposit Range: Minimum & Maximum1
The minimum deposit is ₹1,000, which can be invested in multiples of this amount, and the maximum deposit ceiling is ₹ 30 lakhs. The limit for cash deposits is ₹1 lakh; any amount exceeding this limit must be paid using the bank's payment methods. The deposits can be made from the amount received from the retirement benefits. The sum should be deposited within one month of receiving retirement benefits. All deposits over the ceiling limit are refunded instantly to the account holders. 3. Investment duration1
The scheme has a five-year maturity. Investors have the option of a three-year extension, which gives them flexibility to save further. The extension applications should be done within a year of the maturity date. The transfer of accounts between post offices and banks is also permitted, making it convenient for account holders who may need to relocate during the investment period.
4. Early withdrawal and account closure terms1
Early closing can be done at any time by submitting an application in Form 2; however, penalties are imposed depending on the time of closing. When it is closed within one year, all the interest paid is deducted from the principal amount. A penalty of 1.5% of the principal amount is imposed in case of closure between one and two years, and a 1% penalty applies if you close the account after two years. Pre-mature withdrawal can only be done once, as long as the scheme is active. It is worth noting that, since August 29, 2024, withdrawals made from SCSS accounts are exempt from tax.
5. Facility to add a nominee 1
Account holders are allowed to nominate individuals at the time of account opening or at any time thereafter. This allows easier transfer of proceeds if the depositor passes away. . Benefits of the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme A senior citizen savings scheme is a good retirement investment option because it offers multiple benefits to elderly citizens. Some of these benefits are as follows:
1. Tax-saving benefits1
The investments in SCSS are allowed as tax deductions under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act if you choose the old regime. The maximum deduction is limited to ₹1.5 lakhs in a financial year. The interest received is subject to tax as per the taxpayer's income tax bracket. The proceeds received on premature withdrawal are tax-free starting 29 August , 2024. Tax deducted at source (TDS) will apply to interest income if it exceeds ₹50,000 in a financial year for individuals under 60 and ₹1 lakh for senior citizens.
2. Attractive return rate
The interest rate on the SCSS scheme, effective until 31st March 2026, stands at 8.20% p.a. This competitive rate offers senior citizens attractive returns on their investments. The interest is also reviewed quarterly to factor in market dynamics. 3. Government-secured savings scheme
As a government-backed investment plan, SCSS offers capital protection and guaranteed returns at the time of maturity. 4. Low-risk investment option
SCSS offers predictable and fixed returns. The guaranteed interest rates and capital security make SCSS suitable for elderly investors who may be risk-averse and seek a secure and steady income. Eligibility Criteria of the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme The eligibility criteria of the Senior Citizen Saving Scheme are as follows -
1. Accounts can be opened by Indian citizens who are aged 60 years and older2. 2. Those aged between 55 and 60 years who are retired under the Superannuation, Special
Voluntary Retirement Scheme (VRS), or VRS are also eligible for the scheme2. 3. In the case of retired defence personnel, except civilian employees in the defence sector, the
scheme is available after the completion of 50 years of age if they meet other specified criteria2. 4. Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs) and Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) are not eligible for the
scheme1. 5. The deposit amount ranges from ₹ 1,000 to ₹30 lakhs in multiples of ₹ 1,0001.
6. The eligible retirement benefits that can be deposited include the following1 –
o Gratuity
o Provident Fund o Commuted pension o The saving component of the Group Savings Linked Insurance Scheme o Leave encashment o The benefit of the Employees’ Family Pension Scheme o Ex-gratia payment received from VRS or special VRS Documents Required to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account4 The applicants will be required to submit the following documents:
1. Know your customer (KYC) documents, such as an Aadhaar card, PAN card, voter’s ID card,
or passport. 2. Utility bills, such as electricity or telephone bills, are often used as proof of address. 3. A birth certificate or senior citizen card may be needed in certain situations. 4. The application is to be accompanied by two recent passport-size photographs to finalise the
account opening process.
How to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Account You can open the SCSS account offline. The process is as follows -
1.
o Visit a bank branch or post office of your choice. o Get the official SCSS application form from the counter and fill it out, stating the
details required . o Include self-attested copies of all the mandatory documents and two passport-size
photographs. . Conclusion The Senior Citizen Saving Scheme can help older individuals plan a comfortable life after retirement by creating a source of guaranteed and regular income. Investors can invest their retirement benefits in the scheme and earn attractive returns on a quarterly basis. The tax benefit under Section 80C on investments and the tax exemption on premature withdrawals provide added benefits. Key Takeaways
1. SCSS is a government-backed savings scheme for senior citizens that offers guaranteed and
regular interest income. 2. The one-time deposit amount starts from ₹1000 and goes up to ₹30 lakhs. 3. The maturity period is 5 years and can be extended by up to three years.
4. Some of the benefits of the SCSS scheme include liquidity through premature withdrawal,
guaranteed returns, and tax benefits under Section 80C, among others. FAQs
1. What happens to the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme after the 5-year maturity period?
After the 5-year maturity period, the SCSS account matures, and you can withdraw your deposit. However, you would have the option to extend the scheme's tenure by another 3 years if you apply within one year of the maturity date1.
2. Are there penalty charges for partial withdrawals under SCSS?
Yes, there are penalty charges that depend on when you make the withdrawal. The charges are as follows2 –
o Withdrawal within the first year – Interest paid till withdrawal would be deducted
from the principal amount o Withdrawal after a year but before 2 years - 1.5% of the deposited amount o Withdrawal after two years - 1% of the deposited amount 3. What is the current SCSS interest rate?
The current rate of SCSS interest is 8.2% per annum, which remains valid until 31st March 20262.
4. Can I invest ₹30 lakh in the Senior Citizen Savings Scheme?
Yes, you can invest ₹30 lakhs in SCSS. However, the deposit amount is limited to the retirement benefits received1.

changes.word_original: How to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Account
changes.pdf_final: How to Open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) Account You can open the SCSS account offline.

changes.word_original: You can open the SCSS account both offline and online.
changes.pdf_final: The process is as follows -

changes.word_original: The offline and online processes are explained below -
Offline process of opening a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account
changes.pdf_final: o Visit a bank branch or post office of your choice. o Get the official SCSS application form from the counter and fill it out, stating the

changes.word_original: Visit a bank branch or post office of your choice.
changes.pdf_final: details required . o Include self-attested copies of all the mandatory documents and two passport-size

changes.word_original: Get the official SCSS application form from the counter and fill it out, stating the correct personal and investment-related information.
changes.pdf_final: photographs. .

changes.word_original: Include self-attested copies of all the mandatory documents and two passport-size photographs.
changes.pdf_final: Conclusion The Senior Citizen Saving Scheme can help older individuals plan a comfortable life after retirement by creating a source of guaranteed and regular income.

changes.word_original: Additionally, deposit the amount you wish to invest in the scheme.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: The bank or post office would verify your application and open the SCSS account.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Online process of opening a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme Account
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Eligible investors can open an SCSS account online, provided their bank offers a net banking facility for account opening.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: To open the account -
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Log in to your net banking portal, and under the 'Investment' or 'Savings' menu, select SCSS.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Fill out the online application form with the necessary information
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Attach scanned copies of the necessary documents and deposit the investment amount online
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: After verification, the SCSS account would be opened.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Conclusion
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: The Senior Citizen Saving Scheme can help older individuals plan a comfortable life after retirement by creating a source of guaranteed and regular income.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: So, if you are eligible, you can invest in the scheme to generate a fixed income that can help you manage your expenses post-retirement.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Understand what the SCSS scheme is all about and invest in the savings plan if it aligns with your financial goals.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: SCSS is a government-backed savings scheme for senior citizens that offers guaranteed and regular interest income.
The one-time deposit amount starts from ₹1000 and goes up to ₹30 lakhs.
changes.pdf_final: SCSS is a government-backed savings scheme for senior citizens that offers guaranteed and
regular interest income. 2.
The one-time deposit amount starts from ₹1000 and goes up to ₹30 lakhs. 3.

changes.word_original: Some of the benefits of the SCSS scheme include liquidity through premature withdrawal, guaranteed returns, and tax benefits under Section 80C, among others.

changes.pdf_final: Some of the benefits of the SCSS scheme include liquidity through premature withdrawal,
guaranteed returns, and tax benefits under Section 80C, among others.

changes.word_original: However, you would have the option to extend the scheme's tenure by another 3 years if you apply within one year of the maturity date.
changes.pdf_final: However, you would have the option to extend the scheme's tenure by another 3 years if you apply within one year of the maturity date1.

changes.word_original: The charges are as follows –
changes.pdf_final: The charges are as follows2 –

changes.word_original: Withdrawal within the first year – Interest paid till withdrawal would be deducted from the principal amount
changes.pdf_final: o Withdrawal within the first year – Interest paid till withdrawal would be deducted

changes.word_original: Withdrawal after a year but before 2 years - 1.5% of the deposited amount
changes.pdf_final: from the principal amount o Withdrawal after a year but before 2 years - 1.5% of the deposited amount o Withdrawal after two years - 1% of the deposited amount 3.

changes.word_original: Withdrawal after two years - 1% of the deposited amount
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: The current rate of SCSS interest is 8.2% per annum, which remains valid until 31st March 2026.
changes.pdf_final: The current rate of SCSS interest is 8.2% per annum, which remains valid until 31st March 20262.

changes.word_original: However, the deposit amount is limited to the retirement benefits received.
changes.pdf_final: However, the deposit amount is limited to the retirement benefits received1.
